chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding the Disliked Nature of Traveling in the Month of Ramadan

text: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَدْخُلُ شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ وَ هُوَ مُقِيمٌ لاَ يُرِيدُ بَرَاحاً ثُمَّ يَبْدُو لَهُ بَعْدَ مَا يَدْخُلُ شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ أَنْ يُسَافِرَ فَسَكَتَ فَسَأَلْتُهُ غَيْرَ مَرَّةٍ فَقَالَ «يُقِيمُ أَفْضَلُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ حَاجَةٌ لاَ بُدَّ لَهُ مِنَ اَلْخُرُوجِ فِيهَا أَوْ يَتَخَوَّفَ عَلَى مَالِهِ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1969 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
"I asked him about a man who begins the Month of Ramadan while residing [at home] without any intention to travel. Then, after the month has started, he decides to travel. Imam (as) remained silent. I asked him multiple times, and Imam (as) finally said:
'Staying is better, unless he has a necessary need that compels him to travel or fears for his wealth.'"
